steps.title: Spot the consider-then-rebut structure
steps.content: The phrase “tempting but inaccurate” signals the key move. The underlined portion presents a possible explanation for the Highwaymen's ethereal aesthetic (that fast painting creates it), then immediately rejects this explanation and explains why it's wrong.

steps.title: Match the structure to the right choice
steps.content:
The passage sets up a tempting conclusion (fast painting creates the aesthetic), then rejects it with evidence (Buckner achieved the same look without the technique)—the exact pattern choice B names: “considers and rebuts an interpretation of the effect of a painting technique.”
  • A invents a disagreement never mentioned
  • C falsely claims contrasting aesthetics
  • D reverses the relationship
.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
